message:
Fix CR/CRLF line ending handling in ReadTool, add runtime model fallback in RPC, dedupe symlink aliases in resources, and harden session picker against invalid files

Line ending normalization (src/tools.rs):
- Add normalize_line_endings_chunk() that handles \r\n, \r-only, and
  split-chunk-boundary \r sequences by normalizing to \n during streaming
  reads. This fixes ReadTool offset/limit calculations when files use
  bare CR line endings (classic Mac format) — previously \r was not
  recognized as a line separator, causing incorrect line counting.
- Extend detect_line_ending() to detect bare \r as a distinct line ending
  style (previously treated as LF), enabling correct round-trip preservation.
- Update restore_line_endings() to handle \r restoration.
- Add tests: CR-only offset/limit, split CRLF at 64KB chunk boundary,
  detect_line_ending for \r, EditTool and HashlineEditTool CR preservation.

RPC runtime model fallback (src/rpc.rs):
- Add current_or_runtime_model_entry() that falls back to the runtime
  provider/model when the session header references a provider+model
  combo not found in the available models list. This prevents thinking-
  level clamping, auto-compaction, and model cycling from silently
  failing when the session header is stale or references a removed model.
- Extract model_entry_for_provider_and_id() helper from current_model_entry()
  to share lookup logic between both paths.
- Update all 5 call sites (set_thinking_level, get_thinking_level,
  run_prompt_with_retry context_window, maybe_auto_compact, and
  cycle_model_for_rpc) to use the new fallback.
- cycle_model_for_rpc now resolves the current model entry through the
  fallback before computing the cycle position, fixing cases where a
  missing-header session would always cycle to the first model.
- Add tests for runtime fallback resolution and header-less model cycling.

Session thinking sync refactor (src/interactive/commands.rs):
- Extract plan_session_thinking_sync() pure function that computes
  the effective thinking level, whether it changed, and whether the
  session header needs persistence — replacing inline logic that was
  duplicated between interactive and agent code paths.
- SessionThinkingSyncPlan struct makes the three-way decision (effective
  level, runtime changed, persist needed) explicit and testable.
- Add tests: missing header clamps runtime level, invalid header persists
  correction without false runtime-change signal.

Resource deduplication via canonical paths (src/resources.rs):
- Add canonical_identity_path() helper that resolves symlinks via
  fs::canonicalize for identity comparison. Use it in dedupe_prompts(),
  dedupe_themes(), load_skills(), and ensure_explicit_file_paths_loaded()
  so that symlink aliases pointing to the same underlying file are
  recognized as duplicates instead of generating spurious collision
  diagnostics.
- Add unix-only tests for prompt and theme alias symlink deduplication
  (both inline and in tests/resource_loader.rs integration tests).

Session picker hardening (src/session_picker.rs):
- Introduce ScanSessionsResult struct that separately tracks successfully
  parsed session metas and failed file paths.
- When scan_sessions_on_disk encounters a file that fails build_meta_from_file,
  record it in failed_paths instead of silently ignoring it.
- list_sessions_for_project now evicts cached index rows for sessions
  whose on-disk files have become unparseable (e.g., corrupted headers),
  preventing stale invalid sessions from perpetually appearing in the
  session picker.
- Add test: list_sessions_for_project_evicts_cached_row_when_disk_session_is_invalid
  and build_meta_from_jsonl_rejects_semantically_invalid_header.
- Update existing scan_sessions_on_disk tests for the new return type.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
